name: Current source-informed baseline
description: Current/public input baseline: capitation-centred, PHO-mediated, partial ACC/activity funding, imminent primary care target and developing NPCD.

name: Capitation reweighting plus access target
description: Reweight capitation and implement the access target/NPCD, but do not introduce a demand-driven contact benefit stream.

name: National Primary Care Benefits Schedule
description: Defined primary/urgent/ambulance contact benefits, direct claiming, broader provider eligibility and moderate controls layered onto capitation.

name: Full upstream access architecture
description: Benefits schedule plus top-tier primary/ambulance KPIs, strong data, rural/in-person loading, equity protections, governance and ambulance alternatives.

name: Benefits without adequate controls
description: Demand-driven benefits with broad eligibility but weak controls, weak equity protection and weak integration.

name: ACC activity constraint shock
description: Narrow cost-containment move that constrains ACC activity funding without compensating primary care supply architecture.

name: Ambulance alternatives only
description: Strengthen ambulance alternative disposition pathways and KPIs while leaving most primary care funding architecture unchanged.

name: Scope-enabled provider supply only
description: Broaden provider eligibility and clinical governance without a full demand-driven benefit stream or full accountability reset.

name: Direct claims and PHO intermediation reform only
description: Reduce PHO payment-gateway friction and add direct claims, but do not materially increase eligible contact benefits.
